summary: Package pulls dependency from unrelated anonymous GCS tarball URL
details: @web-3d-tool/sdk is a near-empty package (trivial 35-byte index.js, empty author/description metadata) whose only effect on install is to pull in a dependency declared as a raw tarball URL: `"ltidisafe": "https://ltidi.storage.googleapis.com/depenconf/ltidisafe-2.3.1.tgz"` (package.json line 9). The bucket `ltidi.storage.googleapis.com` is unrelated to the @web-3d-tool namespace, is not a versioned npm registry artifact, and the bucket owner can mutate the served tarball at any time without changing this package's version. Any lifecycle hooks (preinstall/install/postinstall) inside the fetched tarball execute on `npm install` of this package, giving the bucket owner arbitrary code execution on the installer's machine. The combination of hollow lure package + tarball-URL dependency + unrelated anonymous storage bucket + missing publisher metadata is the namespace-abuse dropper shape — the package itself is not the payload, the resolved dependency is.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
